Product Id: The target population for the Canadian Internet use survey has changed from individuals 18 years of age and older in 2005 to individuals 16 years of age and older in 2007.
Residents of Canada excluding residents of the territories, inmates of institutions, persons living on Indian reserves and full-time members of the Canadian Armed Forces.
Electronic commerce: Refers to at least one order and/or one payment made on line for a product or service, for personal or household consumption, during the last twelve months.

Electronic orders: Refers to Internet users who reported to have ordered at least one product or service using the Internet, for personal or household consumption, during the last twelve months, with the payment made or not on Internet. These users may or may not have been window-shoppers.
Number of orders refers to the number of separate orders made on line, total expressed in thousands.
Value of electronic orders expressed in thousand Canadian dollars; average value expressed in dollars.
This CANSIM table has been archived and replaced by the 2010 series of Canadian Internet use tables.
